title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 2. Competency probe: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.

Inspect the Static Runtime Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
